# hyperfluid_secret (Resource)

A managed secret. `value` is write-only — it is never stored in Terraform state. Requires Terraform >= 1.11. Rotate the value by changing `value` together with `value_wo_version`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# `value` is write-only — it is sent to the API but never stored in Terraform
# state. Requires Terraform >= 1.11. To rotate, change `value` together with
# `value_wo_version`. Source the value from a variable, not a literal.
variable "db_password" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}

resource "hyperfluid_secret" "db_password" {
  name             = "db-password"
  secret_type      = "plaintext"
  value            = var.db_password
  value_wo_version = "1"
}

resource "hyperfluid_secret" "config" {
  name             = "app-config"
  secret_type      = "json"
  value            = jsonencode({ feature_flags = { beta = true } })
  value_wo_version = "1"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Secret name. Changing this forces a new secret.
- `secret_type` (String) Secret type: plaintext or json. Changing this forces a new secret.

### Optional

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `description` (String) Free-form description.
- `tags` (List of String) User-defined tags.
- `value` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) Secret material (write-only — never persisted to state). For `json`, a JSON-encoded string. Required on create.
- `value_wo_version` (String) Arbitrary token; change it together with `value` to push a new value (rotation). Needed because the write-only `value` is not tracked in state.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `secret_path` (String) Platform path of the secret.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Secret import id is the secret id (uuid).
# value and value_wo_version are write-only and cannot be recovered on import —
# set them in config afterward.
terraform import hyperfluid_secret.db_password 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
```
